item.title: raiding-raccoon
item.description:
Please find this project on GitHub.

Why

Well… I created this because I was interested in the network that links create on a website. Checking the fastest path from one page to another.
It turned out that a proper graph representation of pages that are linked to another can do really cool things. Especially when using the exported graphml format and loading it into an application like Gephi and then using the ForceAtlas 2 simulation. This creates really interesting insights.

item.title: apollon
item.description:
Sketch

Apollon is a project for classifying tickets in a ticket system into categories (like type, component, labels) before you do it.
Please find this project on GitHub.
The assumption for this to work is that the ticket contains all the necessary information in title and description. Apollon classifies various dates including components, labels, type and priority.

Current state

Currently, only the crawler for training, test and validation data is working. It crawls tickets from GitHub and saves them into AWS S3 buckets (using the AWS ecosystem).
